title: hibbertia echiifolia subsp. rotata
intro: Hibbertia echiifolia subsp. rotata, also known as the hairy Guinea flower, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antifungal activities. Hibbertia echiifolia subsp. rotata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in traditional Australian bush medicine.
